# Transcrição: 19-Matty and the Dog

- **Fonte:** 19-Matty and the Dog
- **Duração:** 05:30
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Thank you. It's a long walk. It's the spring of 2003. I'm 32 years old. I'm sitting in my crummy apartment at a crummy kitchen table. I'm pouring water on a bowl of raisin bran because I am too stupid and too sad to go out and get milk. I am living on my own for the first time ever and I have discovered that when men live on their own they become savages. Like there are nights when I just forget to go to sleep. I find myself like walking through my apartment with underwear and one sock and I can't quite figure out how it happened. And I haven't changed my sheets in the three months I've been here and I don't see him ever changing in the foreseeable future. I hate my apartment. It is small and cruddy and there's an Indian family that lives below me and they're lovely people. But everything they cook is cooked with curry and our air conditioner is stacked one on top of the other. So is the curry blows out of one apartment. It comes into my apartment and I hate curry. So everything I own smells like curry. My clothes and my bed and my dog smells like curry. Less than a mile away my wife is in our kitchen at this time probably the kitchen that I spent three days painting last summer. And she's sitting at a kitchen table that we bought a week after we bought the house or she's sitting on the deck enjoying dinner. It's a deck that I put in two years ago which really means I just paid someone to put it in but then I tell people I put it in. So you think that maybe I was the one who did the work but I still made it happen. And I'm not there because we're separated. About a year ago she told me that she needed space and I didn't really know what that meant. So I started sitting on the other side of the couch and that wasn't good enough for her. And so about six months ago she told me that she was getting bored in the marriage and I didn't know why because I am not boring. I am slightly pedantic and a little organized and slightly OCD but I am not boring. I'm a wedding DJ and my partner Benji and I we met a client that we had done a wedding for a year ago and when we came up to them I said how's it going and they said we bought a dog and we named it after you because you are the funniest person we know. Mind you my DJ's partner name is Benji. They did not choose to name the dog after the man who was named after the dog. They chose to name the dog after me. So I am not boring. But now we're separated and I know it's not really separated. It's like when you like when you're a kid and you discover Santa isn't real but you pretend for one more year to for the parents sake or maybe to get a couple extra presents. That's what we're doing. We're separated but I know what's coming and I hate it. My parents got divorced when I was a kid and it like destroyed my childhood and I swore when I was a kid that I was never going to let this happen to me. That I was going to marry the right person and I was going to work hard at the marriage and it was going to work and now I am in this cruddy apartment and all by myself and I see divorce coming and I've never been depressed in my life but for the first time ever I can like feel the shadows of depression closing in as I see like nothing in my future.

## [03:02 - 05:30]

And I go to Vermont for a weekend to DJ a wedding and when I come back there are these two moving vans right against my house and as I get closer I realize the Indian family is moving out. I see the husband and the wife like trying to get a couch out. Like I've got new neighbors and like they're lovely people the Indian people but I just see like the curry is going away and like it's the first good thing I've had in a long time. And then I see the people moving in their truck is right there too. They're moving and as I get close I realize I know the woman who's standing in that truck. Who is she. And when she sees me coming she smiles like she knows exactly who I am and I'm like oh crap who is this woman who is this woman and then I realize who it is. It's the woman who named her dog after me. And then I see the dog come out from around the truck as she calls my name. It's the dog named after me and it's going to be living below me. This is amazing and then I see her husband coming and then I look and I'm like wait a minute that's not her husband but I know that dude too. How do I know him and they're both coming towards me and they say hi and I'm trying to figure out who he is and then I realize who he is. He's the best man from the wedding. And I look for the husband thinking like he's helping out and she sees me looking around and she says yeah. And I can't believe it. And so we look at each other for a minute we don't say anything and then I say I live upstairs. I'm going to live above the dog that's named after me. If you need any help call me. And so I go up the stairs to my cruddy apartment and I sit down on my cruddy kitchen table and I pour water on Raisin brand and I sit there and I think like there's a person in the world right now maybe sitting in his kitchen and he has lost the wife who I know he loved and he has lost his best friend and he has lost his dog. And I am sitting in my cruddy kitchen but I've got my dog and I'm losing a wife but if I'm honest with myself I'm probably losing a wife who I didn't love enough to begin with. So I think about that guy somewhere in the world who has lost so much and I realize how much I have still and how things might turn out OK after all. Thank you. Thank you.
